title: Epipactis brachiorhynchos
intro: Epipactis brachiorhynchos, also known as the winged helleborine, is a terrestrial orchid species native to Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Epipactis brachiorhynchos include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Epipactis brachiorhynchos is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and tinctures to support respiratory health and alleviate coughs, with its most common herbal preparations being dried leaf infusions and liquid extracts.
taxonomy: I couldn't find any information about Epipactis brachiorhynchos in my database. However, I can tell you about the general classification of the Epipactis genus. The Epipactis genus belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a group of orchids commonly known as helleborines. Within the Orchidaceae family, the Epipactis genus is classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae, specifically within the tribe Neottieae.
